title: List of recognized standards for medical devices - Canada.ca
content:
## List of recognized standards for medical devices
Effective: January 27, 2025 Date adopted: April 11, 2002
Consult the following guidance document on using this list and recognized standards, including version recognition for standards listed without years and editions:
- Guidance on using standards to support compliance with the Medical Device Regulations
- Changes to the list Standards added Standards updated Standards removed
- List of recognized standards Anesthetic and respiratory Biocompatibility Cardiovascular Contraception Dental Electromedical General In vitro diagnostic Manufacturing Materials Neurology Ophthalmology Orthopedics Radiology Sterilization
- Anesthetic and respiratory
- ISO 10993-23: Biological evaluation of medical devices – Part 23: Tests for irritation The following sections (Ed. 1, 2021) are not recognized: Clause 4, General principles – Step-wise approach Clause 6, In vitro irritation tests Annex B, Test method checklist for in vitro irritation testing using reconstructed human epidermis models Annex C, Example of method documentation sheet for reconstructed human epidermis models
- The following sections (Ed. 1, 2021) are not recognized: Clause 4, General principles – Step-wise approach Clause 6, In vitro irritation tests Annex B, Test method checklist for in vitro irritation testing using reconstructed human epidermis models Annex C, Example of method documentation sheet for reconstructed human epidermis models
- Clause 4, General principles – Step-wise approach
- Clause 6, In vitro irritation tests
- Annex B, Test method checklist for in vitro irritation testing using reconstructed human epidermis models
- Annex C, Example of method documentation sheet for reconstructed human epidermis models
- ISO 8637-1: Extracorporeal systems for blood purification – Part 1: Haemodialysers, haemodiafilters, haemofilters and haemoconcentrator
- ISO 11737-3: Sterilization of health care products – Microbiological methods – Part 3: Bacterial endotoxin testing
- CSA Z23500-2: Preparation and quality management of fluids for haemodialysis and related therapies – Part 2: Water treatment equipment for haemodialysis applications and related therapies
- CSA Z23500-3: Preparation and quality management of fluids for haemodialysis and related therapies – Part 3: Water for haemodialysis and related therapies
- CSA Z23500-5: Preparation and quality management of fluids for haemodialysis and related therapies – Part 5: Quality of dialysis fluid for haemodialysis and related therapies
We have removed the years and editions from all recognized standards in this updated list. Only standards updated for other reasons are included.
- ISO 80601-2-61: Medical electrical equipment – Part 2-61: Particular requirements for basic safety and essential performance of pulse oximeter equipment For section 201.12 of this standard (Ed. 2, 2017): accuracy requirements may be tightened compared to the maximum allowable tolerances for accuracy more clinical evidence from a larger group of individuals may be required for pulse oximeter sensors to ensure that validation is available across: a diverse group of individuals with varying skin pigmentation and the full range of oxygenation levels
- For section 201.12 of this standard (Ed. 2, 2017): accuracy requirements may be tightened compared to the maximum allowable tolerances for accuracy more clinical evidence from a larger group of individuals may be required for pulse oximeter sensors to ensure that validation is available across: a diverse group of individuals with varying skin pigmentation and the full range of oxygenation levels
- accuracy requirements may be tightened compared to the maximum allowable tolerances for accuracy
- more clinical evidence from a larger group of individuals may be required for pulse oximeter sensors to ensure that validation is available across: a diverse group of individuals with varying skin pigmentation and the full range of oxygenation levels
- a diverse group of individuals with varying skin pigmentation and
- the full range of oxygenation levels
- ISO 10993-3: Biological evaluation of medical devices – Part 3: Tests for genotoxicity, carcinogenicity and reproductive toxicity For Clause 6.2 (Ed. 3, 2014): in most cases, testing 1 rodent and 1 non-rodent species is required For Clause 7.2 (Ed. 3, 2014): results from a test material would only be considered if the test sample has undergone identical processing steps to the final finished product
- For Clause 6.2 (Ed. 3, 2014): in most cases, testing 1 rodent and 1 non-rodent species is required
- in most cases, testing 1 rodent and 1 non-rodent species is required
- For Clause 7.2 (Ed. 3, 2014): results from a test material would only be considered if the test sample has undergone identical processing steps to the final finished product
- results from a test material would only be considered if the test sample has undergone identical processing steps to the final finished product
- ISO 10993-10: Biological evaluation of medical devices – Part 10: Tests for skin sensitization The following sections (Ed. 4, 2021) are not recognized: Clause 4, General principles – Step-wise approach Annex C, Non-animal methods for skin sensitization
- The following sections (Ed. 4, 2021) are not recognized: Clause 4, General principles – Step-wise approach Annex C, Non-animal methods for skin sensitization
- Clause 4, General principles – Step-wise approach
- Annex C, Non-animal methods for skin sensitization
- ISO 10993-13: Biological evaluation of medical devices – Part 6: Tests for local effects after implantation For Clause 4.1.1, 5.3.4.2, and Figure 1 (Ed. 2, 2010): using the accelerated degradation test as a screening test or a substitute for the real-time degradation test may not be appropriate for all materials and all degradation testing conditions for example, bioabsorbable polymers
- For Clause 4.1.1, 5.3.4.2, and Figure 1 (Ed. 2, 2010): using the accelerated degradation test as a screening test or a substitute for the real-time degradation test may not be appropriate for all materials and all degradation testing conditions for example, bioabsorbable polymers
- using the accelerated degradation test as a screening test or a substitute for the real-time degradation test may not be appropriate for all materials and all degradation testing conditions for example, bioabsorbable polymers
- for example, bioabsorbable polymers
- ISO 5840-1: Cardiovascular implants – Cardiovascular implants – Cardiac valve prostheses – Part 1: General requirements For heart valve substitutes: durability testing may be required beyond 200 million cycles, especially where long-term clinical durability evidence is not available for the indicated patient population
- For heart valve substitutes: durability testing may be required beyond 200 million cycles, especially where long-term clinical durability evidence is not available for the indicated patient population
- durability testing may be required beyond 200 million cycles, especially where long-term clinical durability evidence is not available for the indicated patient population
- IEC 60601-2-24: Medical electrical equipment – Part 2-24: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of infusion pumps and controllers Additional accuracy testing results for flow rates below 1 ml/h may be required depending on the pump's intended use Refer to 'AAMI TIR101 - Fluid Delivery Performance Testing for Infusion Pumps' for recommended delivery performance testing framework and corresponding information to be included in the pump labeling, which align with expectations outlined in the 2018 Health Canada Notice: Changes to Evidence Requirements for Class II and Class III Infusion Pumps
- Additional accuracy testing results for flow rates below 1 ml/h may be required depending on the pump's intended use
- Refer to 'AAMI TIR101 - Fluid Delivery Performance Testing for Infusion Pumps' for recommended delivery performance testing framework and corresponding information to be included in the pump labeling, which align with expectations outlined in the 2018 Health Canada Notice: Changes to Evidence Requirements for Class II and Class III Infusion Pumps

- ISO 11193-2: Single-use medical examination gloves Part 2: Specification for gloves made from poly(vinyl chloride) Text on powdered surgeon's gloves, powdered patient examination gloves and absorbable powder for lubricating a surgeon's glove is not recognized
- Text on powdered surgeon's gloves, powdered patient examination gloves and absorbable powder for lubricating a surgeon's glove is not recognized
- ANSI Z80.7: Ophthalmic optics – Intraocular lenses Refer to ISO 11979-8 for allowable endotoxin levels
- Refer to ISO 11979-8 for allowable endotoxin levels
- ISO 11979-7: Ophthalmic implants – Intraocular lenses – Part 7: Clinical investigations The term "approved" IOL used in this standard should be interpreted as "Health Canada licensed" IOL Annex C Part C.1 may only be accepted if the parent IOL reference has full clinical investigation as per Annex A
- The term "approved" IOL used in this standard should be interpreted as "Health Canada licensed" IOL
- Annex C Part C.1 may only be accepted if the parent IOL reference has full clinical investigation as per Annex A
- AIUM/NEMA UD 2: Acoustic output measurement standard for diagnostic ultrasound equipment
- AIUM/NEMA UD 3: Standard for real-time display of thermal and mechanical acoustic output indices on diagnostic ultrasound equipment
- IEC 61000-3-2: Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) – Part 3-2: Limits – Limits for harmonic current emissions (equipment input current ≤ 16 A per phase)
- IEC 61000-3-3: Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) – Part 3-3: Limits – Limitation of voltage changes, voltage fluctuations and flicker in public low-voltage supply systems, for equipment with rated current ≤16A per phase and not subject to conditional connection
- IEC 61000-4-2: Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) – Part 4-2: Testing and measurement techniques – Electrostatic discharge immunity test
- IEC 61000-4-3: Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) – Part 4-3: Testing and measurement techniques – Radiated, radio-frequency, electromagnetic field immunity test
- IEC 61000-4-4: Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) – Part 4-4: Testing and measurement techniques – Electrical fast transient/burst immunity test
- IEC 61000-4-5: Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) – Part 4-5: Testing and measurement techniques – Surge immunity test

- ISO 13959: Water for haemodialysis and related therapies
- ISO 26722: Water treatment equipment for haemodialysis applications and related therapies
